generated_comment: These days every big company wants one woman on top for optics, so naturally she got fast-tracked. I am not saying she is incapable, but real leadership needs a tough mind, and usually when a woman reaches that level she has to become almost like a man to handle it.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines a woman's promotion as driven by tokenism rather than merit and frames competent leadership as inherently masculine. It encodes the surface manifestations of 'diversity hire' suspicion and masculinization as a backhanded compliment.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 47. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at your cousin's wedding sitting at the men's table during dinner. Someone brings up that a well known female politician in your state who is unmarried and childless just won a big election. The men at the table are reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on whether a woman who has no husband and no children can truly understand the needs of a family and whether she deserves the public's trust and respect.
